project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.
current_milestone: M2 — Evidence Log skeleton (S3.1 aios-evidence crate)
current_task_title: T-014 queued — privacy ceiling on Query §10 trailer count
current_step: T-013 closed. Redaction profiles per S3.1 §14 landed: closed RedactionProfile enum (Default/Strict/DebugCapture, serde wire names default/strict/debug_capture matching §14 table verbatim). Per-RecordType policy via record_category() prefix-matching over the 427-entry wire vocabulary into 6 categories (Lifecycle/Vault/Recovery/Telemetry/Segment/Generic). Strict reduces Lifecycle/Telemetry/Generic to structural marker {record_type, payload_hash_prefix (BLAKE3-truncated), redacted_under_profile:'strict'}; Vault hashes PII keys, keeps capability metadata; Recovery/Segment kept verbatim. Default/DebugCapture share secret-redaction body transformation (spec §14 — both 'minimal redaction; only secrets'); operationally distinguished by the receipt's redaction_profile field. Constitutional pipeline: apply_redaction() runs in seal_inner BEFORE content_hash + Ed25519 signature — no unredacted-but-signed representation exists anywhere. ReceiptForSigning includes redaction_profile so tampering with profile post-seal breaks verify_signature. EvidenceReceipt.redaction_profile uses #[serde(default)] for back-compat with pre-T-013 receipt JSON. Proto AppendRequest NOT mutated (per STOP rule); server applies Default automatically. Proto receipt envelope field tag 14 now populated with the lowercase wire name. New files: redaction.rs (~550 LoC, 16 unit tests), tests/redaction_roundtrip.rs (5 integration tests). Workspace 271 → 306 tests (+35). All gates: build/test/clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings/fmt --check PASS.
completed_steps_M1: T-001 .. T-006 — M1 closed at 102 tests
completed_steps_M2: T-007 .. T-011 — see prior memory snapshot

T-013: redaction profiles per S3.1 §14. New crates/aios-evidence/src/redaction.rs (~550 LoC). Closed RedactionProfile enum with #[default] = Default; wire names default/strict/debug_capture exactly match §14 spec table. Per-RecordType policy via record_category() routes the 427-entry RecordType vocabulary into 6 categories (Lifecycle/Vault/Recovery/Telemetry/Segment/Generic) by prefix-matching as_wire_str() — avoids a 427-arm match while staying coherent with the spec's own namespace-coded vocabulary. Three transformation primitives: redact_keys (substring match on normalize_key()-ed name → REDACTED_SENTINEL value), hash_pii_keys (PII keys → hash:<32hex-truncated>), reduce_to_structural_marker (Lifecycle/Telemetry/Generic under Strict → {record_type, payload_hash_prefix, redacted_under_profile:'strict'}). Default+DebugCapture share the secret-only body transformation by spec (both 'minimal redaction; only secrets') — distinguished operationally via the receipt's stored redaction_profile field. Strict additionally: hashes PII keys on Vault payloads (capability metadata preserved, subject ids hashed), reduces Lifecycle/Telemetry/Generic to structural marker, keeps Recovery/Segment verbatim (structural by design). normalize_key() handles hyphen/space/dot/camelCase variants. Receipt integration: EvidenceReceipt now has a redaction_profile field with #[serde(default)] for back-compat with pre-T-013 receipt JSON. ReceiptBuilder has with_redaction_profile() setter; default = RedactionProfile::Default. seal_inner applies apply_redaction() BEFORE computing content_hash so the cryptographic chain witnesses the redacted form — constitutional anchor §14 ('no unredacted-but-signed representation anywhere'). ReceiptForSigning includes the profile so tampering breaks signature verification. Proto receipt envelope field tag 14 (redaction_profile) populated with the lowercase wire name; AppendRequest NOT mutated per the STOP rule, so the gRPC server applies Default automatically. New integration test tests/redaction_roundtrip.rs covers: strict envelope serializes/parses/verifies (incl. profile preservation), default keeps non-sensitive fields, debug_capture preserves PII but strips obvious secrets, strict vs default produce distinct content hashes (proof signed bytes depend on profile), gRPC append wire envelope carries 'default'. 16 unit tests in redaction.rs (profile vocabulary, serde round-trip, per-profile pattern coverage incl. password/token/api_key/private_key/credentials/case-insensitive/headers-with-hyphen/arrays/null+empty inputs/non-mutation guarantee). 10 new t013_* tests in receipt.rs (builder default, with_redaction_profile setter, seal applies redaction before content_hash, seal_signed signs over redacted payload, strict reduces lifecycle payload, re-encoded parsed receipt preserves content_hash, legacy receipt without field deserializes with Default, tampering profile breaks signature, debug_capture round-trip, seal_signed with Strict verifies). Workspace 271 → 306 tests (+35). All gates: cargo build/test --workspace + clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings + fmt --check PASS.

T-012: RocksDB persistence backend per S3.1 §7.2 / §11.4. rocksdb 0.23 (default-features=false, features=['snappy','lz4']) bundles librocksdb-sys 0.17.3+10.4.2 with RocksDB 10.4.2 source — compiles cleanly under GCC 15 (the 0.22 / RocksDB-8.10 combo previously failed on missing <cstdint> includes; Governor verified 0.23 builds in 2m 08s on this dev box). tempfile 3.10 dev-dep for hermetic test fixtures. New module crates/aios-evidence/src/persistence/ with 4 files: encoding.rs (CF names + key encoders — 283 LoC), recovery.rs (OpenSegmentSnapshot — 147 LoC), rocksdb_backend.rs (RocksDbEvidenceLog impl — 1320 LoC after clippy/fmt + lint-driven refactor), mod.rs (public surface — 35 LoC). 6 column families: receipts (key: raw evr_<ULID> bytes; value: JSON EvidenceReceipt), segments (key: raw seg_<32hex>; value: JSON SealedSegment), current_segment (single-row 'current' key; value: JSON OpenSegmentSnapshot), metadata (sentinel keys for chain_head, schema_version, log_id), chain_index (key: u64 big-endian chain position; value: seg_<32hex> ASCII bytes), record_type_index (composite key: u16_be record-type wire id + i64_be recorded-at nanos + evr_ULID ASCII trailer; value: empty). Big-endian keys mean lexical = numeric ordering for chain walks; the composite record_type_index key supports a per-record-type, time-range, single prefix sweep. WAL discipline: db_opts.set_wal_recovery_mode(AbsoluteConsistency) refuses to start on WAL corruption (the default PointInTime mode silently truncates — would violate evidence-survives-power-loss). Every WriteBatch is committed with WriteOptions::set_sync(true) for fsync-on-write. Append is one atomic WriteBatch touching receipts + record_type_index + current_segment; seal_current_segment is one batch touching segments + chain_index + metadata::chain_head + current_segment (cleared). Crash recovery on open: (1) open_raw_db with all 6 CFs auto-created; (2) validate_or_stamp_schema_version (aios.evidence.v1alpha1); (3) restore_or_mint_log_id (stable across restarts, persisted in metadata); (4) read_chain_head from metadata; (5) recover_sealed_chain walks 0..chain_head, fetches each SealedSegment from segments CF, runs verify_full (per-receipt signatures + per-segment seal hash + Ed25519 seal signature), then validates cross-segment linkage (previous_segment_seal_hash matches prior segment's segment_seal_hash + previous_segment_id matches); (6) restore_open_snapshot decodes the OpenSegmentSnapshot and runs ReceiptChain::verify_integrity over its receipts. Tamper or chain break surfaces as ChainBroken / SegmentChainBroken / SegmentSealMismatch / SegmentSignatureMismatch — propagated to the caller. The original 114-line open() was split into 5 helpers (open_raw_db, validate_or_stamp_schema_version, restore_or_mint_log_id, read_chain_head, restore_open_snapshot) to satisfy clippy::too_many_lines without an allow. RocksDbEvidenceLog implements the same proto::evidence_log_server::EvidenceLog trait as InMemoryEvidenceLog — tonic server bootstrap unchanged; the production binary picks the backend by config. Manual Debug impl (DB is not Debug). New public SealedSegment::is_compaction_eligible(now: DateTime<Utc>) -> bool implements S3.1 §11.5 / §13 retention windows (STANDARD_24M = 730 days, EXTENDED_60M = 1825 days, FOREVER never); T-012 surfaces eligibility only — actual deletion is T-015 per the task brief. Integration tests: tests/persistence_crash_recovery.rs (5 tests: ten_receipts+seal_survives_restart, open_segment_snapshot_survives_restart_without_seal, tampered_sealed_segment_on_disk_is_rejected_at_reopen, log_id_is_stable_across_restart, verify_chain_matches_pre_restart_state); tests/grpc_roundtrip_persistent.rs (4 tests: grpc_evidence_log_full_roundtrip_over_rocksdb, grpc_subscribe_replays_then_streams_live_over_rocksdb, receipts_persisted_via_grpc_survive_restart, grpc_append_rejects_unspecified_record_type_and_empty_subject_over_rocksdb). Unit tests inside rocksdb_backend.rs (14 tokio-async tests covering open, append, read_receipt, query, verify_chain, rebuild_index, get_log_info, seal_current_segment, schema-version refusal, chain reopen, log_id persistence, rocksdb_property). Plus 5 retention-eligibility tests in segment.rs and 8 encoding tests + 3 recovery tests. Workspace total: 230 → 271 (+41). cargo build/test/clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings/fmt --check all PASS.
discovered_constraints_T012: rocksdb 0.23 / librocksdb-sys 0.17.3+10.4.2 / vendored RocksDB 10.4.2: compiles cleanly under GCC 15 + clang 22 + bindgen 0.72. First cold build takes ~3m 53s; warm rebuilds reuse the librocksdb-sys artifact and finish in seconds. This is the AIOS pin for every future crate that needs embedded storage.
RocksDb's DB is not Debug — derive(Clone) is fine via Arc<DB> but Debug requires a manual impl. We render log_id + started_at + live_subscribers and call finish_non_exhaustive.
tonic generated trait impls trigger clippy::result_large_err (tonic::Status is 176 bytes). Scope an allow on the impl block. Same pattern as T-011.

clippy::significant_drop_tightening fires on a tokio RwLock guard held across a sync I/O call (rocksdb write). For the seal path the guard MUST stay held — the on-disk atomic batch and the in-memory state update need to happen as one logical step to prevent a concurrent append from seeing the cleared snapshot before the chain_head is bumped. Scope the allow with a reason at the function level.

Splitting RocksDbEvidenceLog::open into helpers (open_raw_db, validate_or_stamp_schema_version, restore_or_mint_log_id, read_chain_head, restore_open_snapshot, recover_sealed_chain) is the simplest way to satisfy clippy::too_many_lines on the open path while keeping each helper testable in isolation.

clippy::doc_markdown is strict in this crate: bare PascalCase identifiers (RocksDB, GetLogInfo, AbsoluteConsistency) MUST be backticked in doc comments — and so must bare camelCase / SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE words.

rocksdb 0.23 API: ColumnFamilyDescriptor::new(name, opts) + DB::open_cf_descriptors(&opts, path, descriptors). get_cf / put_cf_opt / write_opt are stable. WriteOptions::set_sync(true) is the per-write fsync flag; pair with db_opts.set_wal_recovery_mode(AbsoluteConsistency) for the AIOS WAL discipline.
tempfile 3.27 (auto-resolved from `tempfile = '3.10'`): TempDir is dropped recursively. Hermetic test isolation works out of the box.

When the rocksdb iterator yields `Result<(Box<[u8]>, Box<[u8]>), Error>`, the test code may use `iter.next().expect('row').expect('ok')` to unwrap both layers — typical pattern for direct raw-DB inspection in integration tests.

Adding `clippy::too_many_lines` to an allow attribute at the test crate's #![allow(...)] block is the documented workaround for long integration tests with the full-roundtrip pattern (T-011 lesson, repeated here).

When validating SealedSegment::previous_segment_id against a running prev_segment_id during recovery, use `match (Option<&T>, &Option<T>) { (Some(p), Some(expected)) if p == expected => Ok(...), _ => err }` — the deref/cmp is what `==` on PartialEq does for SegmentId.
notes: T-013 closed. T-014 (privacy ceiling on Query §10 trailer count) queued next. No env prereqs — pure Rust on top of the existing in-memory + RocksDB backends.
